behaviors.description: Clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) in a contract
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is extraction quality — whether the model actually finds all key terms. This is not a module-boundary bug; it is an AI capability question. REAL-INFRA: No real OS or network needed. LLM-DEP: Whether all parties, dates, obligations, and penalties are correctly identified depends entirely on what the real LLM produces. A stub that always returns a fixed extraction would pass trivially, hiding real recall failures. Running 100 times with a real model can yield different results. MULTI-STEP: Only one reviewer step. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: no sequential agentic steps; this is a single reviewer call.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real contract containing at least two parties, three dates, two obligations, and one penalty clause to the live clause extractor. Assert that every ground-truth key term is present in the returned extraction. Run across several representative contracts to surface recall failures that only a real model reveals.

behaviors.description: Risk assessor flags problematic language even when phrased indirectly or euphemistically
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is the model's inability to interpret euphemistic or indirect language — a purely AI comprehension quality issue, not a module-wiring bug. REAL-INFRA: No subprocess or network primitives needed. LLM-DEP: Whether the assessor flags 'mutual adjustment of obligations' as a liability cap, or 'best-efforts basis' as an unenforceable commitment, depends on the real LLM's semantic understanding. A mock always returns a predetermined answer and can never surface this failure. MOCK-HIDE: Any mocked reviewer would trivially pass regardless of phrasing. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: single reviewer step, no multi-step sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Construct contracts where high-risk clauses are expressed in indirect or euphemistic language (e.g., 'indemnification obligations may be adjusted at either party's discretion'). Submit each to the live risk assessor and assert that each euphemistic risk clause appears in the flagged output. Vary phrasing across test cases to probe the model's paraphrase robustness.

behaviors.description: Compliance checker correctly determines whether a contract meets regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is the AI's incorrect or incomplete application of jurisdiction-specific regulations — not a code logic or module-wiring bug. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess primitives needed. LLM-DEP: Whether a contract correctly satisfies GDPR Article 28 processor clauses vs. CCPA requirements is a question of the real model's regulatory knowledge and reasoning. Outcomes can differ across runs. A stub or mock cannot reproduce a real compliance reasoning failure. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: single reviewer, no multi-step chaining.
behaviors.test_description: For at least two jurisdictions (e.g., EU/GDPR and US/CCPA), create one compliant and one deliberately non-compliant contract. Submit each to the live compliance checker with the corresponding jurisdiction config. Assert that compliant contracts are accepted and non-compliant contracts are rejected with the correct regulatory references cited.

behaviors.description: Merge step preserves both conflicting assessments with attribution when two reviewers disagree on the same clause
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is the merge algorithm dropping or overwriting one reviewer's output when two assessments conflict on the same clause. This is a deterministic data-handling bug at the boundary between reviewer outputs and the merge module, not an AI quality issue. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network/subprocess needed; reviewer outputs can be injected as fixed structs. LLM-DEP: The merge step's conflict-preservation rule is deterministic code; correctness does not depend on LLM non-determinism. A controlled pair of conflicting structured outputs is sufficient to trigger and observe the failure. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic: the behavior does not say the merge step uses an LLM; it describes a structural rule ('preserves both assessments with attribution'). Escalating to Agentic requires disqualifying evidence that the merge uses a real model.
behaviors.test_description: Construct two pre-built reviewer result objects for the same clause — one with assessment 'standard' attributed to the clause extractor and one with 'high risk' attributed to the risk assessor. Feed both into the merge module directly. Assert that the unified report contains both assessments, each attributed to its source reviewer, with neither overwritten or silently dropped.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline — dispatch to all three reviewers, collect results, merge — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any reviewer's findings
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode spans the entire pipeline: dispatch, parallel execution of three reviewers, result collection, and merge. Failures can appear at any handoff — a reviewer result silently dropped during collection, a race condition in parallel dispatch, or a merge that omits one reviewer's output. None of these are catchable by testing a single module boundary. REAL-INFRA: No real OS primitives required beyond the in-process pipeline. LLM-DEP: 'Completes correctly' means all three real AI reviewers produce meaningful findings, not empty stubs; correctness depends on LLM output. MULTI-STEP: Three independent agentic reviewer calls followed by a merge step constitutes multiple agentic steps in sequence (or parallel) — exactly the Workflow definition. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic (single step): this behavior explicitly covers all three reviewers plus the merge step; it is structurally multi-step.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real contract to the full pipeline end-to-end with all three live reviewers enabled. Collect the unified report and assert: (a) the report contains findings attributed to each of the three reviewers, (b) no reviewer's section is empty or missing, and (c) any clause present in multiple reviewers' raw outputs appears in the merged report with correct attribution. Run at least twice to surface non-deterministic omissions.
